focus_points.label: Strategic scope vs tactical delivery tradeoff
focus_points.reason: Ahmed talks about senior scope but redirects into tactical delivery details. One possibility is temporary pressure; another is a confidence gap around visibility and influence at his level—worth clarifying what’s driving the shift right now.

focus_points.label: Where stakeholder trust feels thin
focus_points.reason: Ahmed’s note points to potential gaps in visibility and influence. Whether it’s Product, Engineering, or exec peers, you want to map the specific trust gaps he sees so the growth plan targets the relationships that unlock next-level scope.

focus_points.label: How he defines the next-level Head role
focus_points.reason: What he thinks “next level” means is the anchor for a growth plan that’s concrete. For a Head of Product, you want his definition of strategic ownership, decision rights, and outcomes so you can turn it into measurable steps.

focus_points.label: Where he still acts as an individual executor
focus_points.reason: If he’s consistently defaulting to delivery, delegation and leverage are likely the skill to build next. This conversation should identify the specific areas he should hand off or co-own so he can spend time on strategic decisions rather than execution details.
